description: Mesoamerican materials, pigments, surface treatments, and their visual properties for generative AI rendering reference.

stone_materials.name: Jade (Jadeite / Nephrite / Serpentine)
stone_materials.surface: High-gloss polished; slight translucency in thin sections; mottled/banded internal veining
stone_materials.cultural_value: Highest value material in all Mesoamerican cultures; more precious than gold; associated with water, maize, life, breath
uses: mosaics inlaid in wooden or resin substrates
stone_materials.render_hints: Deep green with slight blue-grey undertone; high specular highlight on surface; subsurface scattering creates depth; veining is white-grey diagonal bands; maximum saturation in deepest shadow

stone_materials.name: Obsidian
stone_materials.surface: Mirror-polished; near-black with reflective sheen; knife-edge fractures are razor-sharp and translucent at thin edges
stone_materials.cultural_value: Sacred and utilitarian; sacrificial blades, prismatic blades for bloodletting, mirror discs, spear and dart points
stone_materials.render_hints: Near-black (#0B0C10) with very strong specular highlight at correct angle; almost mirror-like; thin edges lighten to dark grey-brown; conchoidal fracture pattern at edges

stone_materials.name: Basalt
stone_materials.surface: Rough exterior from weathering; carved surfaces are smoother but still coarse-grained; not polished
stone_materials.render_hints: Dark grey with fine grain texture visible at close range; slight brownish warmth in some specimens; carved channels appear darker from shadow and grime accumulation

stone_materials.name: Limestone (Caliza)
stone_materials.surface: Relatively smooth; good carving medium; weathers to grey-cream surface; original surfaces often plastered and painted
stone_materials.render_hints: Warm grey-cream; surface weathers to lighter tone; carved channels and relief areas show slightly darker tone from trapped sediment; original surfaces had plaster and pigment which shows in traces

stone_materials.name: Travertine / Tecali (Mexican Onyx)
stone_materials.surface: Semi-translucent when backlit; banded with white and yellow layers; smooth polished
stone_materials.render_hints: Honey-yellow with white banding; semi-translucent quality; warm glow when light passes through thin sections

stone_materials.name: Serpentine
stone_materials.surface: Smooth; waxy luster; mottled green with grey-black veins
uses: secondary greenstone for celts and offerings
stone_materials.render_hints: Medium green with grey-black veining; waxy rather than glossy; softer appearance than jadeite

stone_materials.name: Andesite / Volcanic Porphyry
stone_materials.surface: Medium-grain; reddish to grey-brown; harder than limestone; used for Aztec sculpture
stone_materials.render_hints: Warm grey-red; visible phenocryst crystals at close range; carved surfaces smooth but with slight texture

pigments.name: Maya Blue (Azul Maya)
pigments.composition: Indigo (from Indigofera suffruticosa) + attapulgite/palygorskite clay; unique organo-mineral compound
pigments.properties: Extraordinarily durable (survives 1000+ years; acid-resistant); medium-bright blue; slightly grey-blue
pigments.render_hints: Medium-value blue; slightly desaturated/dusty compared to modern paint; not as vivid as cobalt; matte to slight sheen; survives as even tone on walls

pigments.name: Cinnabar (Mercury Sulfide / Vermilion)
pigments.properties: Brilliant, saturated red; extremely toxic (mercury); prestigious precisely because of its rarity and toxicity
uses: elite burial pigment (painted over corpses and objects)
pigments.render_hints: Pure vivid red-orange; highly saturated; appears fresh even in ancient contexts due to chemical stability; brilliant specular in mineral form

pigments.name: Cochineal (Cochenille / Grana Cochinilla)
pigments.composition: Carminic acid extracted from Dactylopius coccus insects on prickly pear cactus
pigments.properties: Deep red to crimson; can shift to purple with mordant variation; less stable than cinnabar over centuries
pigments.render_hints: Deep warm crimson; slightly cooler/darker than cinnabar; ages to brownish-red in codex contexts; vivid when fresh

pigments.name: Carbon Black (Lamp Black / Charcoal)
pigments.composition: Carbon from charred bone, lamp black, or charcoal
uses: all outline work in codex and mural painting
pigments.render_hints: Absolute black; matte; slight warm undertone in charcoal; cold/blue-black in lamp black

pigments.name: Yellow Ochre (Limonite / Jarosite)
pigments.composition: Hydrated iron oxide (limonite); or jarosite (potassium iron sulfate)
pigments.render_hints: Warm medium yellow with brown undertone; earthy, natural quality; not vivid chrome yellow

pigments.name: Red Ochre / Hematite
pigments.render_hints: Deep warm red; slightly brick or rust toned; common and widely used; appears everywhere in ancient contexts

pigments.name: Indigo (Añil)
pigments.render_hints: Deep blue-indigo; cooler and darker than Maya Blue; slight purple undertone; appears as blue-black in dense applications

pigments.name: Malachite Green / Mineral Green
pigments.composition: Copper carbonate hydroxide; also vegetal greens from plant extracts
pigments.render_hints: Middle-value green; slight blue tint; matte in malachite mineral form

pigments.name: Turquoise Blue-Green
pigments.render_hints: Bright, vivid cyan-blue-green; medium value; more green-shifted than Maya Blue; glassy luster in polished stone

pigments.name: Iron Pyrite (Fool's Gold)
pigments.render_hints: Metallic gold-yellow; high specular; mirror-like in polished form; warm yellow metallic sheen

pigments.name: Calcined Bone / Lime White
pigments.composition: Calcium carbonate from limestone or calcined bone
pigments.render_hints: Warm off-white; slight cream or ivory tone; matte; slightly textured at close range

surface_treatments.name: Fresco Secco (Dry Fresco)
surface_treatments.description: Pigment applied to dried lime plaster surface; primary mural technique in Teotihuacan, Bonampak, and other sites
surface_treatments.visual_quality: Flat, matte surface; colors can be vivid but without gloss; slight absorbency effect in some areas
surface_treatments.render_hints: Matte; slight surface texture from plaster; colors may have slight dust/bloom at surface; edges of applied color are slightly soft where pigment absorbed

surface_treatments.name: Burnished Ceramic / Stone Polish
surface_treatments.description: Stone-polished ceramic surface creating a low-to-high gloss; also stone objects polished with abrasives
surface_treatments.visual_quality: Low to mirror sheen depending on intensity; thin-walled ceramics with very smooth surface
surface_treatments.render_hints: Smooth surface with directional highlight; slight sheen angled with surface direction; less harsh than modern gloss glaze

surface_treatments.name: Modeled Stucco
surface_treatments.description: Lime plaster applied in layers; modeled into three-dimensional relief while wet; then painted
surface_treatments.visual_quality: 3D surface with painted color; surface slightly rough at texture scale; deep carving channels are darker
surface_treatments.render_hints: Combine relief height with painted color; micro-texture of plaster surface; raking light reveals relief depth; pigment collects in deepest channels (pigment-in-recesses effect)

surface_treatments.name: Stone-Incised Relief / Bas-Relief
surface_treatments.description: Forms cut into stone surface with chisels; range from shallow incision to deep undercutting
surface_treatments.visual_quality: Stone surface with carved channels; chisel marks visible in rougher work; smooth in high-quality carving
surface_treatments.render_hints: Use ambient occlusion in channels; raking directional light from low angle (as if lit by torchlight or side sun); carved edge has slight chipped micro-texture; deep channels are darkest; surface ridges catch brightest light

surface_treatments.name: Mosaic Inlay (Turquoise / Jade / Pyrite)
surface_treatments.description: Small stone tiles (tesserae) affixed to substrate with organic adhesive; creates colored geometric or figural images
surface_treatments.visual_quality: Grid of small tiles; slight height variation between tiles; grout lines between tiles; highly colorful
surface_treatments.render_hints: Tile grid visible at close range; each tile a slightly different shade/orientation; grout lines as fine dark channels; high-specular on polished stone tiles; overall surface has irregular but rhythmic geometry
